prerequisites: A GoLogin account (sign up at gologin.com).
A GoLogin API token generated from your account dashboard.
A start_date value marking the earliest date from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: GoLogin authenticates every API request with a single API token sent as an HTTP Bearer header of the form 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Provide this value in the connector's api_key field. The token is created and copied from your GoLogin dashboard.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to GoLogin
setupSteps.body: Create or log in to your GoLogin account. The API token is tied to your account, so any profiles and data the connector reads will belong to it.

setupSteps.title: Generate your API token
setupSteps.body: Open your GoLogin dashboard and go to the API token page at https://app.gologin.com/personalArea/TokenApi. Generate (or copy) your token there. GoLogin also exposes a POST /user/dev endpoint to create additional dev tokens programmatically, which themselves require an existing Bearer token to call.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token in api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied token into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' on each request to the GoLogin REST API. Keep the token secret and rotate it if it is exposed.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Enter the required start_date. The connector uses this as the earliest point from which to pull records, so only data on or after this date is synced.

sources.title: GoLogin API - Create dev token (Bearer authentication)
